pairs.en: Wednesday, Apr. 29, 2026 at 5:30 p.m. ET
pairs.zh: 2026 年 4 月 29 日星期三下午 5:30ET

pairs.en: Call participants
pairs.zh: 呼叫参与者

pairs.en: Chairman and Chief Executive Officer — Satya Nadella
pairs.zh: 董事长兼首席执行官 — 萨蒂亚·纳德拉

pairs.en: Chief Financial Officer — Amy Hood
pairs.zh: 首席财务官 - 艾米·胡德

pairs.en: Chief Accounting Officer — Alice Jolla
pairs.zh: 首席会计官 — 艾丽丝·乔拉

pairs.en: Deputy General Counsel and Corporate Secretary — Brian Defoe
pairs.zh: 副总法律顾问兼公司秘书 — Brian Defoe

pairs.en: Head of Investor Relations — Jonathan Neilson
pairs.zh: 投资者关系主管 — 乔纳森·尼尔森

pairs.en: Revenue-- $82.9 billion, up 1815% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 收入——829 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 1815%。

pairs.en: Operating Income-- Increased 2016% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 营业收入——按固定汇率计算，2016 年增长 %。

pairs.en: Earnings Per Share-- $4.27, up 218% in constant currency when adjusted for investment in OpenAI.
pairs.zh: 每股收益——4.27 美元，根据对 OpenAI 的投资进行调整后，按固定汇率计算增长 218%。

pairs.en: Gross Margin Percentage-- 68%, down year over year due to continued AI investment, partially offset by efficiency gains.
pairs.zh: 毛利率——68%，由于持续的人工智能投资而同比下降，但部分被效率提升所抵消。

pairs.en: Operating Expenses-- Increased 98% in constant currency, primarily reflecting AI investments in R&D, compute, and talent.
pairs.zh: 运营支出——按固定汇率计算增长 98%，主要反映了在研发、计算和人才方面的人工智能投资。

pairs.en: Headcount-- Declined year over year as focus remains on operational agility.
pairs.zh: 员工人数同比下降，因为重点仍然放在运营敏捷性上。

pairs.en: Cash Flow from Operations-- $46.7 billion, up 26% driven by strong cloud billings and collections.
pairs.zh: 运营现金流——467 亿美元，在强劲的云账单和收款推动下增长 26%。

pairs.en: Free Cash Flow-- $15.8 billion, impacted by higher capital expenditures.
pairs.zh: 自由现金流——158 亿美元，受到资本支出增加的影响。

pairs.en: Capital Expenditures-- $31.9 billion, with two-thirds spent on short-lived assets (GPUs and CPUs); finance leases total $4.7 billion.
pairs.zh: 资本支出——319 亿美元，其中三分之二用于短期资产（GPU 和 CPU）；融资租赁总额为 47 亿美元。

pairs.en: Shareholder Returns-- $10.2 billion returned through dividends and share repurchases.
pairs.zh: 股东回报——通过股息和股票回购获得 102 亿美元回报。

pairs.en: Commercial Bookings-- Grew 7% excluding OpenAI impact; declined 46% including Azure commitments from OpenAI.
pairs.zh: 商业预订——增长 7%（不包括 OpenAI 的影响）；包括 OpenAI 的 Azure 承诺在内，下降了 46%。

pairs.en: Commercial Remaining Performance Obligation (RPO)-- $627 billion, up 99% year over year (including OpenAI); 25% recognized as revenue over the next 12 months, up 39%.
pairs.zh: 商业剩余履约义务 (RPO)——6270 亿美元，同比增长 99%（包括 OpenAI）；25% 确认为未来 12 个月的收入，增长 39%。

pairs.en: Microsoft Cloud Revenue-- $54.5 billion, grew 2925% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 微软云收入——545 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 2925%。

pairs.en: Productivity and Business Processes Segment Revenue-- $35 billion, up 1713% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 生产力和业务流程部门收入——350 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 1713%。

pairs.en: Microsoft 365 Commercial Cloud Revenue-- Up 1915% in constant currency; paid seats surpassed 20 million and grew 6% year over year.
pairs.zh: Microsoft 365 商业云收入——按固定汇率计算增长 1915%；付费座位突破2000万个，同比增长6%。

pairs.en: Microsoft 365 Consumer Cloud Revenue-- Grew 3329% in constant currency; subscriptions increased 7%.
pairs.zh: Microsoft 365 消费者云收入——按固定汇率计算增长 3329%；订阅量增加了 7%。

pairs.en: LinkedIn Revenue-- Increased 129% in constant currency across all lines of business; members reached 1.3 billion.
pairs.zh: LinkedIn 收入——按固定汇率计算，所有业务线增长了 129%；会员达到13亿。

pairs.en: Dynamics 365 Revenue-- Increased 2217% in constant currency, reflecting continued share gains and broad workload growth.
pairs.zh: Dynamics 365 收入 - 按固定汇率计算增长 2217%，反映出持续的份额增长和广泛的工作负载增长。

pairs.en: Intelligent Cloud Segment Revenue-- $34.7 billion, up 3028% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 智能云部门收入——347 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 3028%。

pairs.en: Azure and Other Cloud Services Revenue-- Grew 4039% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: Azure 和其他云服务收入——按固定汇率计算增长 4039%。

pairs.en: More Personal Computing Segment Revenue-- $13.2 billion, declined 1% (down 3% in constant currency).
pairs.zh: 更多个人计算部门收入为 132 亿美元，下降 1%（按固定汇率计算下降 3%）。

pairs.en: Windows OEM and Devices Revenue-- Decreased 23% in constant currency; Windows OEM rose slightly and was ahead of expectations due to inventory build and memory price increases.
pairs.zh: Windows OEM 和设备收入——按固定汇率计算下降 23%；由于库存增加和内存价格上涨，Windows OEM 小幅上涨并超出预期。

pairs.en: Search Advertising Revenue (ex-TAC)-- Increased 129% in constant currency, driven by Edge and Bing growth; Bing monthly active users reached 1 billion.
pairs.zh: 搜索广告收入（不包括 TAC）——在 Edge 和 Bing 增长的推动下，按固定汇率计算增长了 129%；Bing月活跃用户达到10亿。

pairs.en: Gaming Revenue-- Decreased 79% in constant currency; Xbox content and services revenue down 57% as prior year was bolstered by first-party content.
pairs.zh: 博彩收入——按固定汇率计算下降 79%；受第一方内容的推动，Xbox 内容和服务收入较上年下降 57%。

pairs.en: Microsoft Cloud Gross Margin-- 66%, slightly better than expected but down year over year due to AI investment.
pairs.zh: 微软云毛利率——66%，略好于预期，但由于人工智能投资而同比下降。

pairs.en: AI Business-- Annual revenue run rate surpassed $37 billion, up 123%.
pairs.zh: AI业务——年营收运行率超过370亿美元，增长123%。

pairs.en: Copilot Paid Seats-- Surpassed 20 million, increased 250% year over year; monthly engagement at Outlook levels.
pairs.zh: 副驾驶付费座位——突破2000万个，同比增长250%；Outlook 级别的每月参与度。

pairs.en: GitHub Copilot Enterprise Adoption-- Nearly 140,000 organizations, tripled year over year; Copilot CLI usage almost doubled month over month.
pairs.zh: GitHub Copilot 企业采用率——近 140,000 个组织，同比增长两倍；Copilot CLI 使用率逐月几乎翻倍。

pairs.en: Fabric Customer Growth-- 35,000 paid customers (up 60%), and OneLake data increased nearly 4x.
pairs.zh: Fabric 客户增长 - 35,000 名付费客户（增长 60%），OneLake 数据增长近 4 倍。

pairs.en: Database Growth-- Cosmos DB revenue up 50% with AI app workload demand.
pairs.zh: 数据库增长——随着 AI 应用工作负载需求的增长，Cosmos DB 收入增长了 50%。

pairs.en: AI Model Innovations-- MAI Transcribe One (67% GPU efficiency gain) and MAI Image Two (up to 260% efficiency gain); both powering first-party and select commercial offerings.
pairs.zh: AI模型创新——MAI Transcribe One（GPU效率提升67%）和MAI Image Two（效率提升高达260%）；两者都为第一方和精选商业产品提供支持。

pairs.en: Commercial Q4 Outlook-- Expect Productivity and Business Processes segment revenue of $37.0-$37.3 billion (12%-13% growth) and Intelligent Cloud segment revenue of $37.95-$38.25 billion (27%-28% growth); More Personal Computing projected at $11.75-$12.25 billion.
pairs.zh: 商业第四季度展望——预计生产力和业务流程部门收入为 37.0-373 亿美元（增长 12%-13%），智能云部门收入为 37.95-382.5 亿美元（增长 27%-28%）；更多个人计算预计将达到 11.75 至 122.5 亿美元。

pairs.en: Capital Expenditures Outlook-- Over $40 billion in Q4, with expectation to reach $190 billion for calendar year 2026 including $25 billion in higher component pricing.
pairs.zh: 资本支出展望——第四季度超过 400 亿美元，预计 2026 年将达到 1,900 亿美元，其中组件定价上涨 250 亿美元。

pairs.en: Operating Margins Guidance-- Fiscal year 2026 margins expected to increase by approximately one point year over year, despite investment and one-time retirement costs (~$900 million in Q4).
pairs.zh: 营业利润指导——尽管存在投资和一次性退休成本（第四季度约为 9 亿美元），但 2026 财年的利润率预计将同比增长约 1 个百分点。

pairs.en: Azure Capacity and Supply Constraints-- Supply expected to remain constrained at least through 2026; Azure growth rates projected to modestly accelerate in the second half of calendar 2026.
pairs.zh: Azure 容量和供应限制——供应预计至少到 2026 年仍将受到限制；Azure 增长率预计将在 2026 年下半年小幅加速。

pairs.en: OpenAI Partnership Agreement Update-- CFO Hood said, "having the revenue share exist through 2030—the predictability of that is a real positive for us—and, as Satya pointed out, the IP being royalty-free with the elimination of our rev share to them."
pairs.zh: OpenAI 合作伙伴协议更新——首席财务官 Hood 表示，“收入分成一直持续到 2030 年——这种可预测性对我们来说是一个真正的积极因素——而且，正如 Satya 指出的那样，IP 是免版税的，因为我们不再向他们提供收入分成。”

pairs.en: Microsoft(MSFT+5.25%)reported record results with rapid year-over-year growth in revenue, operating income, and EPS, supported by surging cloud and AI demand. Management highlighted material expansion in paid Copilot seats, increased product usage intensity, and a transformative shift in enterprise monetization toward hybrid seat-plus-consumption models. Guidance signals elevated capital investments to address persistent infrastructure constraints and maintain high service availability amid robust commercial demand.
pairs.zh: 微软（MSFT+5.25%）公布了创纪录的业绩，在云和人工智能需求激增的支持下，收入、营业收入和每股收益同比快速增长。管理层强调了付费副驾驶座位的实质性扩张、产品使用强度的增加以及企业货币化向座位加消费混合模式的变革性转变。指导意见表明，将增加资本投资，以解决持续存在的基础设施限制，并在强劲的商业需求中保持高服务可用性。

pairs.en: Executives articulated that future revenue streams will increasingly blend per-user entitlements with usage-based consumption rather than traditional seat licensing alone.
pairs.zh: 高管们表示，未来的收入来源将越来越多地将每用户的权利与基于使用的消费结合起来，而不仅仅是传统的席位许可。

pairs.en: Chairman Nadella stated, "it—customers want predictability for budgets and procurement, and the seat-based pricing is just entitlement to some consumption. That is the way to think about."
pairs.zh: 纳德拉董事长表示，“客户希望预算和采购具有可预测性，而基于席位的定价只是获得一些消费的权利。这就是思考的方式。”

pairs.en: CFO Hood explained the revised OpenAI agreement gives Microsoft royalty-free IP access through 2032 and sustained revenue sharing through 2030, which management described as positive for long-term predictability.
pairs.zh: 首席财务官 Hood 解释说，修订后的 OpenAI 协议使微软能够在 2032 年之前获得免版税的 IP 访问权限，并在 2030 年之前持续分享收入，管理层称这对长期可预测性具有积极意义。

pairs.en: Capital spending will remain heavily weighted toward short-term compute assets, with leadership confident in ROI due to booked business, capacity investments, and observed monetization trends.
pairs.zh: 资本支出仍将重点关注短期计算资产，由于预订业务、容量投资和观察到的货币化趋势，领导层对投资回报率充满信心。

pairs.en: Management asserted the next year will see further headcount reductions and operating expense growth limited to the mid- to high single digits as efficiency initiatives accelerate.
pairs.zh: 管理层声称，随着效率举措的加速，明年将进一步裁员，运营费用增长将限制在中高个位数。

pairs.en: Industry glossary
pairs.zh: 行业术语

pairs.en: ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue): The yearly value of contracted repeatable revenues normalized to a 12-month period, reflecting ongoing subscription and consumption activity.
pairs.zh: ARR（年度经常性收入）：标准化为 12 个月期间的合同可重复收入的年度值，反映持续的订阅和消费活动。

pairs.en: Copilot: Microsoft’s suite of generative AI product offerings, including productivity, coding, and security agents, sold both as seat licenses and usage-based consumption.
pairs.zh: Copilot：微软的生成式人工智能产品套件，包括生产力、编码和安全代理，以席位许可证和基于使用的消费形式出售。

pairs.en: Ex-TAC: Revenue from advertising after deducting traffic acquisition costs paid to strategic partners or affiliates.
pairs.zh: Ex-TAC：扣除支付给战略合作伙伴或附属公司的流量获取成本后的广告收入。

pairs.en: Foundry: Microsoft cloud platform enabling customers to deploy, customize, and scale AI models and agents across various workflow scenarios.
pairs.zh: Foundry：Microsoft 云平台使客户能够跨各种工作流程场景部署、自定义和扩展 AI 模型和代理。

pairs.en: OneLake: Microsoft Fabric’s central data storage platform aggregating operational and analytical data for enterprise AI and business analytics workloads.
pairs.zh: OneLake：Microsoft Fabric 的中央数据存储平台，聚合企业人工智能和业务分析工作负载的操作和分析数据。

pairs.en: Agentic Systems: AI-powered software entities (agents) capable of performing delegated, long-running tasks autonomously across domains like productivity and security.
pairs.zh: 代理系统：人工智能驱动的软件实体（代理）能够跨领域（例如生产力和安全性）自主执行委托的、长期运行的任务。

pairs.en: RPO (Remaining Performance Obligation): Value of undelivered contractual future revenues from active commercial bookings, to be recognized as revenue over time.
pairs.zh: RPO（剩余履约义务）：来自活跃商业预订的未交付合同未来收入的价值，随着时间的推移被确认为收入。

pairs.en: Azure Boost: Microsoft’s custom silicon and networking acceleration technology to optimize cloud infrastructure efficiency and performance.
pairs.zh: Azure Boost：微软的定制芯片和网络加速技术，用于优化云基础设施的效率和性能。

pairs.en: Full Conference Call Transcript
pairs.zh: 完整的电话会议记录

pairs.en: Jonathan Neilson:And thank you for joining us today. On the call with me are Satya Nadella, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer; Amy Hood, Chief Financial Officer; Alice Jolla, Chief Accounting Officer; and Brian Defoe, Deputy General Counsel and Corporate Secretary. On the Microsoft Investor Relations website, you can find our earnings press release and financial summary slide deck, which is intended to supplement our prepared remarks during today's call and provide the reconciliation of differences between GAAP and non-GAAP financial measures. More detailed outlook slides will be available on the Microsoft Investor Relations website when we provide outlook commentary on today's call. On this call, we will discuss certain non-GAAP items.
pairs.zh: 乔纳森·尼尔森：感谢您今天加入我们。与我通话的有董事长兼首席执行官萨蒂亚·纳德拉 (Satya Nadella)；艾米·胡德，首席财务官；艾丽丝·乔拉，首席会计官；以及副总法律顾问兼公司秘书 Brian Defoe。在微软投资者关系网站上，您可以找到我们的收益新闻稿和财务摘要幻灯片，其旨在补充我们在今天的电话会议中准备好的言论，并提供 GAAP 和非 GAAP 财务指标之间差异的调节。当我们对今天的电话会议提供展望评论时，将在微软投资者关系网站上提供更详细的展望幻灯片。在这次电话会议上，我们将讨论某些非公认会计准则项目。

pairs.en: The non-GAAP financial measures provided should not be considered as a substitute for, or superior to, the measures of financial performance prepared in accordance with GAAP. They are included as additional clarifying items to aid in further understanding the company's third quarter performance in addition to the impact these items and events have on the financial results. All growth comparisons we make on the call today relate to the corresponding period of last year unless otherwise noted. We will also provide growth rates in constant currency, when available, as a framework for assessing how our underlying businesses performed, excluding the effect of foreign currency rate fluctuations.
pairs.zh: 所提供的非公认会计原则财务指标不应被视为替代或优于根据公认会计原则编制的财务业绩指标。它们作为额外的澄清项目包括在内，除了这些项目和事件对财务业绩的影响之外，还有助于进一步了解公司第三季度的业绩。除非另有说明，我们今天在电话会议上进行的所有增长比较都与去年同期有关。我们还将提供以固定货币计算的增长率（如果有），作为评估我们的基础业务表现的框架，排除外币汇率波动的影响。

pairs.en: Where growth rates are the same in constant currency, we will refer to the growth rate only. We will post our prepared remarks to our website immediately following the call until the complete transcript is available. Today's call is being webcast live and recorded. If you ask a question, it will be included in our live transmission, in the transcript, and in any future use of the recording. You can replay the call and view the transcript on the Microsoft Investor Relations website. During this call, we will be making forward-looking statements, our predictions, projections, or other statements about future events. These statements are based on current expectations and assumptions that are subject to risks and uncertainties.
pairs.zh: 如果按固定汇率计算的增长率相同，我们将仅参考增长率。我们将在通话后立即将准备好的评论发布到我们的网站上，直到获得完整的文字记录。今天的电话会议正在进行网络直播并进行录音。如果您提出问题，它将包含在我们的实时传输、文字记录以及未来录音的任何使用中。您可以重播通话并在 Microsoft 投资者关系网站上查看文字记录。在这次电话会议中，我们将做出前瞻性陈述、我们的预测、预测或有关未来事件的其他陈述。这些陈述基于当前的预期和假设，存在风险和不确定性。

pairs.en: Actual results could materially differ because of factors discussed in today's earnings press release, the comments made during this conference call, and in the Risk Factors section of our Forms 10-K, Forms 10-Q, and other reports and filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. We do not undertake any duty to update any forward-looking statement. And with that, I will turn the call over to Satya.
pairs.zh: 由于今天的收益新闻稿中讨论的因素、本次电话会议期间发表的评论以及我们的 10-K 表格、10-Q 表格以及其他报告和向美国证券交易委员会提交的文件中的风险因素部分，实际结果可能会存在重大差异。我们不承担更新任何前瞻性声明的义务。然后，我会将电话转给 Satya。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Thank you very much, Jonathan. It was a record third quarter powered by the continued strength of Microsoft Cloud, which delivered $54 billion in revenue, up 29% year over year. Our AI business surpassed $37 billion ARR, up 123%. We are at the beginning of one of the most consequential platform shifts that will change the entire tech stack as agents proliferate and become the dominant workload. This will drive TAM mix and change the value creation equation across the entire economy. To capture this opportunity, we are executing against two priorities. First, we are building the world's leading cloud and AI infrastructure for the agentic computing era.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：非常感谢你，乔纳森。在微软云持续强劲的推动下，第三季度创下了创纪录的业绩，该季度收入达到 540 亿美元，同比增长 29%。我们的人工智能业务年收入超过 370 亿美元，增长 123%。我们正处于最重要的平台转变之一的开始，随着代理的激增并成为主要工作负载，这将改变整个技术堆栈。这将推动 TAM 组合并改变整个经济的价值创造方程。为了抓住这个机会，我们正在执行两个优先事项。首先，我们正在为代理计算时代构建全球领先的云和人工智能基础设施。

pairs.en: Second, we are building high-value agentic systems across core domains such as productivity, coding, and security. These two layers reinforce each other, and we are focused on driving competitive value and differentiation for customers across each so that they can maximize their outcomes. Today, I will focus my remarks on both priorities starting with infrastructure. We are optimizing every layer of the tech stack from data center design to silicon to system software, the model architecture, as well as its optimization. This is translating into operational gains. We have reduced doctor lifetimes for new GPUs in our big regions by nearly 20% since the beginning of the year.
pairs.zh: 其次，我们正在跨生产力、编码和安全等核心领域构建高价值的代理系统。这两层相互促进，我们致力于为每一层客户推动竞争价值和差异化，以便他们能够最大限度地提高成果。今天，我将从基础设施开始重点讨论这两个优先事项。我们正在优化技术堆栈的每一层，从数据中心设计到芯片再到系统软件、模型架构及其优化。这正在转化为运营收益。自今年年初以来，我们已将大区域新 GPU 的医生寿命缩短了近 20%。

pairs.en: Our Fairwater data center in Wisconsin came online earlier this month, six weeks ahead of schedule, allowing us to recognize revenue earlier. And we delivered a 40% improvement in inference throughput for our most used models across Copilot, driven by our software and hardware optimization work. All up, we added another gigawatt of capacity this quarter and remain on track to double our overall footprint in just two years. We are moving aggressively to add capacity aligned to the demand signals we see, and we have announced new data center investments across four continents. We also continue to modernize our fleet with our first-party innovation alongside the latest from NVIDIA and AMD.
pairs.zh: 我们位于威斯康星州的费尔沃特数据中心于本月早些时候上线，比原计划提前六周，使我们能够更早地确认收入。在我们的软件和硬件优化工作的推动下，我们在 Copilot 中最常用的模型的推理吞吐量提高了 40%。总而言之，我们本季度又增加了十亿瓦的容量，并有望在短短两年内将我们的总体足迹翻一番。我们正在根据我们看到的需求信号积极增加容量，并且我们已经宣布在四大洲进行新的数据中心投资。我们还继续利用第一方创新以及 NVIDIA 和 AMD 的最新技术对我们的机队进行现代化改造。

pairs.en: Across our fleet, millions of servers are powered by our custom networking, security, and virtualization silicon, including Azure Boost, as well as our first-party CPUs and accelerators. Our Maya 200 AI accelerator, with over 30% improved tokens per dollar compared to the latest silicon in our fleet, is now live in our Iowa and Arizona data centers. Our Cobalt server CPU is deployed in nearly half of our data center regions running workloads at scale for customers like Databricks, Siemens, and Snowflake. As our largest customers scale their AI deployments, they are increasingly leveraging other services across our platform and choosing to run those workloads on Cobalt. And we are expanding Cobalt supply significantly to meet this demand.
pairs.zh: 在我们的机群中，数百万台服务器由我们的自定义网络、安全和虚拟化芯片（包括 Azure Boost）以及我们的第一方 CPU 和加速器提供支持。我们的 Maya 200 AI 加速器与我们最新的芯片相比，每美元的代币成本提高了 30% 以上，现已在爱荷华州和亚利桑那州的数据中心投入使用。我们的 Cobalt 服务器 CPU 部署在近一半的数据中心区域，为 Databricks、Siemens 和 Snowflake 等客户大规模运行工作负载。随着我们最大的客户扩展其人工智能部署，他们越来越多地利用我们平台上的其他服务，并选择在 Cobalt 上运行这些工作负载。我们正在大幅扩大钴供应以满足这一需求。

pairs.en: The next layer up from infrastructure is the agent app platform. It starts with model choice. We offer the broadest selection of models of any hyperscaler so customers can choose the right model for the right workload across OpenAI, Anthropic, open source, and more. Over 10,000 customers have used more than one model on Foundry, 5,000 have used open source models, and the number who have used Anthropic and OpenAI models increased 2x quarter over quarter. For example, Bayer is using multiple models in Foundry to create its own in-house agent platform with more than 20,000 active monthly users.
pairs.zh: 基础设施的下一层是代理应用程序平台。它从模型选择开始。我们提供最广泛的超大规模模型选择，以便客户可以针对 OpenAI、Anthropic、开源等领域的正确工作负载选择正确的模型。超过 10,000 名客户在 Foundry 上使用了多个模型，5,000 名客户使用了开源模型，使用 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 模型的数量环比增长了 2 倍。例如，拜耳正在 Foundry 中使用多种模型来创建自己的内部代理平台，每月活跃用户超过 20,000 名。

pairs.en: All up, over 300 customers are on track to process over 1 trillion tokens on Foundry this year, accelerating 30% quarter over quarter. We also remain focused on our first model work to differentiate our high-value copilots and agents and reduce COGS. We introduced MAI Transcribe One, a state-of-the-art speech-to-text model, and MAI Image Two, one of the top image generation models in the world. These models are already powering first-party scenarios like image generation in Bing and PowerPoint, and we are working towards having Transcribe One power transcription in Copilot and Teams. Early signals show a 67% increase in GPU efficiency with Transcribe One and up to a 260% increase in Image Two.
pairs.zh: 总而言之，今年将有超过 300 家客户在 Foundry 上处理超过 1 万亿个代币，季度环比增长 30%。我们还继续专注于我们的第一个模型工作，以区分我们的高价值副驾驶和代理并降低销售成本。我们推出了 MAI Transcribe One（一种最先进的语音转文本模型）和 MAI Image Two（世界顶级图像生成模型之一）。这些模型已经为第一方场景提供了支持，例如 Bing 和 PowerPoint 中的图像生成，我们正在努力在 Copilot 和 Teams 中提供 Transcribe One 强大的转录功能。早期信号显示，Transcribe One 的 GPU 效率提高了 67%，Image Two 的 GPU 效率提高了 260%。

pairs.en: We also brought MAI models to commercial customers like Shutterstock and WPP for the first time through Foundry. And we are innovating on OpenAI IP to drive product evals and lower COGS. Two recent examples are what we have done with step retrieval with WorkIQ and Copilot and how reasoning adapts to intent complexity in Researcher with much reduced latency and increased accuracy. The next layer up is all about enterprise data and context. Across Fabric, Foundry, Microsoft 365, and our security graph, we are building a unified IQ layer for organizational intelligence. Thousands of enterprises already are accessing context across these IQ layers.
pairs.zh: 我们还通过 Foundry 首次将 MAI 模型带给 Shutterstock 和 WPP 等商业客户。我们正在 OpenAI IP 上进行创新，以推动产品评估并降低 COGS。最近的两个例子是我们使用 WorkIQ 和 Copilot 进行步骤检索，以及推理如何适应 Researcher 中的意图复杂性，同时大大减少延迟并提高准确性。下一层是关于企业数据和上下文的。跨 Fabric、Foundry、Microsoft 365 和我们的安全图，我们正在为组织智能构建统一的 IQ 层。成千上万的企业已经在跨这些 IQ 层访问上下文。

pairs.en: And as AI usage grows, so does the context layer, creating a flywheel that continuously improves the grounding, relevance, and effectiveness of every agent they use and build, making our IQ layers an unmatched context engine for organizational intelligence. More broadly, our database business accelerated quarter over quarter. Cosmos DB alone saw 50% year-over-year revenue growth driven by AI app workloads. We now have 35,000 paid Fabric customers, up 60% year over year. And the amount of data in Fabric OneLake increased nearly 4x year over year. Over 15,000 customers now use both Foundry and Fabric, up 60% year over year, as enterprises connect agents to real-time operational, analytical, and unstructured data that Fabric brings together.
pairs.zh: 随着人工智能使用量的增长，上下文层也在增长，从而创建一个飞轮，不断提高他们使用和构建的每个代理的基础、相关性和有效性，使我们的 IQ 层成为组织智能的无与伦比的上下文引擎。更广泛地说，我们的数据库业务逐季加速增长。在 AI 应用工作负载的推动下，Cosmos DB 的收入同比增长了 50%。我们现在拥有 35,000 名 Fabric 付费客户，同比增长 60%。Fabric OneLake 中的数据量同比增长了近 4 倍。现在，随着企业将代理连接到 Fabric 汇集的实时操作、分析和非结构化数据，超过 15,000 家客户同时使用 Foundry 和 Fabric，同比增长 60%。

pairs.en: And we are very excited about the continued progress with Foundry Agents Service and how customers can now build durable, stateful agents that run across time boundaries, orchestrate tools and models, and close the loop with evals and improvement over long-running workflows. Beyond Fabric and Foundry, we are also helping knowledge workers build agents with tools like Copilot Studio. Nearly 90% of the Fortune 500 now have agents built with our low-code, no-code tools, and we are seeing fast growth of our Copilot credit-consumptive offer, up nearly 2x quarter over quarter, as customers increasingly extend Copilot with custom agents tailored to their workflows.
pairs.zh: 我们对 Foundry Agents Service 的持续进展以及客户现在如何构建跨时间边界运行的持久、有状态的代理、编排工具和模型以及通过评估和改进长期运行的工作流程来关闭循环感到非常兴奋。除了 Fabric 和 Foundry 之外，我们还帮助知识工作者使用 Copilot Studio 等工具构建代理。近 90% 的财富 500 强企业现在都拥有使用我们的低代码、无代码工具构建的代理，随着客户越来越多地使用针对其工作流程定制的自定义代理来扩展 Copilot，我们看到 Copilot 信用消耗产品的快速增长，季度环比增长了近 2 倍。

pairs.en: Finally, with Agent 365, we offer a control plane that extends companies' existing governance, identity, security, and management frameworks to agents. Tens of thousands of companies are already managing tens of millions of agents in Agent 365, and we expect this momentum to grow significantly as agents will increasingly need tools for identity, governance, security, and more. Now let me turn to the high-value agentic systems we ourselves are building on this platform. We are evolving our family of Copilots from synchronous assistants to async co-workers that can execute long-running tasks across key domains. In knowledge work, it was another record quarter for Microsoft 365 Copilot seat adds, which increased 250% year over year, representing our fastest growth since launch.
pairs.zh: 最后，通过 Agent 365，我们提供了一个控制平面，将公司现有的治理、身份、安全和管理框架扩展到代理。数以万计的公司已经在 Agent 365 中管理数千万个代理，我们预计这种势头将显着增长，因为代理将越来越需要身份、治理、安全等工具。现在让我谈谈我们自己在这个平台上构建的高价值代理系统。我们正在将 Copilot 系列从同步助理发展为异步同事，可以跨关键领域执行长时间运行的任务。在知识工作方面，Microsoft 365 Copilot 席位增加量在本季度又创历史新高，同比增长 250%，这是我们自推出以来最快的增长速度。

pairs.en: Quarter over quarter, we continue to see acceleration and now have over 20 million Microsoft 365 Copilot paid seats. The number of customers with over 50,000 seats quadrupled year over year, and Accenture now has over 740,000 seats, our largest Copilot win to date. And Bayer, Johnson & Johnson, Mercedes, and Roche all committed to 90,000 or more seats. Copilot is uniquely valuable at work where nearly every task depends on organizational context. WorkIQ grounds Copilot responses in the full context of an organization, including people, roles, documents, and communications, all within the company's security boundary. The system of work behind WorkIQ alone spans more than 17 exabytes of data, growing 35% year over year.
pairs.zh: 每个季度，我们都继续看到加速增长，现在 Microsoft 365 Copilot 付费席位已超过 2000 万。拥有超过 50,000 个座位的客户数量同比翻了两番，埃森哲现在拥有超过 740,000 个座位，这是我们迄今为止最大的副驾驶胜利。拜耳、强生、梅赛德斯和罗氏都承诺提供 90,000 个或更多座位。副驾驶在几乎每项任务都取决于组织环境的工作中具有独特的价值。WorkIQ 将 Copilot 响应置于组织的完整环境中，包括人员、角色、文档和通信，所有这些都在公司的安全范围内。仅 WorkIQ 背后的工作系统就涵盖了超过 17 艾字节的数据，同比增长 35%。

pairs.en: The liquidity and freshness of that data matters, with billions of emails, documents, and chats, hundreds of millions of Teams meetings, and millions of SharePoint sites added each day. And that context is getting even richer as Copilot grows; Copilot and agent conversations and artifacts they create feed back into WorkIQ, making it even more context rich. We continue to increase the pace of feature innovation across Microsoft 365 Copilot, introducing over 625 updates over the past year, up 50%. In Microsoft 365 Copilot, you now have access in chat to multiple models by default with intelligent auto-routing. In Agents, with Critique and Counsel, you can use multiple models together to generate optimal responses.
pairs.zh: 这些数据的流动性和新鲜度非常重要，每天都会添加数十亿封电子邮件、文档和聊天、数亿个 Teams 会议以及数百万个 SharePoint 网站。随着 Copilot 的成长，这种背景变得更加丰富。副驾驶和座席对话以及他们创建的工件反馈到 WorkIQ 中，使其上下文更加丰富。我们继续加快 Microsoft 365 Copilot 的功能创新步伐，在过去一年中推出了超过 625 个更新，增长了 50%。在 Microsoft 365 Copilot 中，您现在可以通过智能自动路由默认在聊天中访问多个模型。在 Agents 中，通过 Critique 和 Counsel，您可以一起使用多个模型来生成最佳响应。

pairs.en: As of last week, agent mode is now the default experience across Copilot in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. And with CoWork, you now have a new way to delegate and complete work using Copilot. All this innovation is driving record usage intensity across Copilot. We have seen a surge in usage of our first-party agents, with monthly usage up 6x year to date. Copilot queries per user were up nearly 20% quarter over quarter. To put this momentum in perspective, weekly engagement is now at the same level as Outlook, as more and more users make Copilot a habit.
pairs.zh: 截至上周，代理模式现已成为 Copilot 在 Word、Excel 和 PowerPoint 中的默认体验。借助 CoWork，您现在拥有了一种使用 Copilot 委派和完成工作的新方法。所有这些创新正在推动 Copilot 的使用强度创历史新高。我们看到第一方代理的使用量激增，今年迄今为止的月使用量增长了 6 倍。每个用户的 Copilot 查询量环比增长了近 20%。从这一势头来看，随着越来越多的用户使 Copilot 成为一种习惯，每周的参与度现在与 Outlook 处于同一水平。

pairs.en: When it comes to business applications, we are seeing a new pattern emerge as customers shift from the traditional seat model to seats plus consumption. The customer service category is at the forefront of this transformation, as nearly 60% of our service customers are already purchasing usage-based credits. For example, HSBC uses prebuilt agents with Dynamics 365 to manage customer inquiries across products, markets, and regulatory requirements, reducing resolution time by over 30%. And our agentic products in LinkedIn Talent Solutions, which help hirers automate time-consuming tasks like sourcing, screening, and drafting messages, have already surpassed a $450 million annualized revenue run rate.
pairs.zh: 在商业应用方面，我们看到一种新的模式正在出现，客户从传统的座位模式转向座位加消费。客户服务类别处于这一转型的最前沿，因为我们近 60% 的服务客户已经购买了基于使用情况的积分。例如，汇丰银行使用 Dynamics 365 预构建代理来管理跨产品、市场和监管要求的客户查询，将解决时间缩短 30% 以上。我们在 LinkedIn Talent Solutions 中的代理产品可帮助招聘者自动执行耗时的任务，例如采购、筛选和起草消息，年收入运行率已超过 4.5 亿美元。

pairs.en: When it comes to developers, GitHub itself is seeing unprecedented growth driven by the proliferation of agentic coding, and we are hard at work to scale and meet this demand. We see this even with GitHub Copilot. Nearly 140,000 organizations now use GitHub Copilot in Enterprise, nearly tripled year over year. The majority of users leverage multiple models. We are also seeing rapid adoption of GitHub Copilot CLI, with usage nearly doubling month over month. And earlier this week, we announced our move to a usage-based pricing model for GitHub Copilot as we align pricing to actual usage and cost. When it comes to security, the physics of cybersecurity has changed as AI compresses the window between vulnerability and exploitation.
pairs.zh: 就开发人员而言，在代理编码激增的推动下，GitHub 本身正在经历前所未有的增长，我们正在努力扩展并满足这一需求。即使使用 GitHub Copilot，我们也能看到这一点。现在有近 140,000 个组织在 Enterprise 中使用 GitHub Copilot，同比增长近两倍。大多数用户利用多种模型。我们还看到 GitHub Copilot CLI 的快速采用，使用量逐月几乎翻倍。本周早些时候，我们宣布将 GitHub Copilot 转向基于使用情况的定价模型，使定价与实际使用情况和成本保持一致。在安全方面，随着人工智能压缩漏洞和利用之间的窗口，网络安全的物理原理已经发生了变化。

pairs.en: To help mitigate risk immediately, we sim-ship Defender protections when updates for AI-discovered vulnerabilities are released. And we are on course to productize new multimodal AI-driven scanning harnesses as well. Already, the number of Security Copilot customers increased 2x year over year. Our data security triage agents alone handled over 2 million unique alerts this quarter. And we are helping customers secure their AI deployments as well. Thirty-five billion Copilot interactions have been audited by Purview to date, up 7x year over year. Finally, when it comes to our consumer business, we are doing the foundational work required to win back fans and strengthen engagement across Windows, Xbox, Bing, and Edge.
pairs.zh: 为了帮助立即降低风险，当人工智能发现的漏洞更新发布时，我们会提供 Defender 保护。我们还将生产新的多模式人工智能驱动的扫描线束。Security Copilot 客户数量已经同比增加了 2 倍。本季度仅我们的数据安全分类代理就处理了超过 200 万个独特警报。我们还帮助客户保护他们的人工智能部署。迄今为止，Purview 已审核了 350 亿次 Copilot 交互，同比增长 7 倍。最后，在我们的消费者业务方面，我们正在开展必要的基础工作，以赢回粉丝并加强 Windows、Xbox、Bing 和 Edge 的参与度。

pairs.en: In the near term, we are focused on fundamentals, prioritizing quality, and serving our core users better. You see this in the work underway across our consumer products. With Windows, we recently announced performance improvements for lower-memory devices, streamlined the Windows Update experience, and brought back focus to core features and fundamentals that matter most to our customers. And you also see this in Xbox, where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players and shaping the future of play. Last week's Game Pass changes are one example of how we are staying close to customer feedback. Monthly active Windows devices surpassed 1.6 billion, and over time, Windows value will extend to deliver unmetered intelligence at the edge.
pairs.zh: 短期内，我们将聚焦基础、质量优先，更好地服务核心用户。您可以在我们针对消费产品正在进行的工作中看到这一点。对于 Windows，我们最近宣布了对低内存设备的性能改进，简化了 Windows 更新体验，并将重点重新集中到对客户最重要的核心功能和基础知识上。您在 Xbox 中也能看到这一点，团队正在重新致力于我们的核心粉丝和玩家，并塑造游戏的未来。上周的游戏通行证更改就是我们如何密切关注客户反馈的一个例子。每月活跃的 Windows 设备超过 16 亿台，随着时间的推移，Windows 的价值将扩展到在边缘提供无限的智能。

pairs.en: Our Edge browser has taken share for twenty consecutive quarters, and Bing monthly active users reached 1 billion for the first time. LinkedIn has 1.3 billion members, and we are seeing increased depth of conversation, and it is the leading B2B sales and advertising channel for large and small businesses. We set new records for monthly Xbox users in the quarter as well as game streaming hours, and in Microsoft 365 Consumer, we now have nearly 95 million subscribers, and early signals show increasing satisfaction as we make agent mode the default. Across everything I have talked about, we are also hard at work changing the way we work.
pairs.zh: 我们的Edge浏览器已经连续二十个季度占据市场份额，Bing月活跃用户首次达到10亿。LinkedIn 拥有 13 亿会员，我们看到对话深度不断增加，它是大型和小型企业领先的 B2B 销售和广告渠道。我们在本季度创下了 Xbox 月用户数以及游戏流媒体时长的新纪录，在 Microsoft 365 Consumer 中，我们现在拥有近 9500 万订阅者，并且随着我们将代理模式设置为默认模式，早期信号显示满意度不断提高。在我所谈到的一切中，我们也在努力改变我们的工作方式。

pairs.en: Our north star remains the same: delivering customer value with the highest quality and top-class innovation, and this is what gives me confidence in our ability to shape the next phase of growth for our company and our customers. With that, let me turn it over to Amy to walk through our financial results and outlook.
pairs.zh: 我们的北极星始终如一：以最高的质量和一流的创新为客户提供价值，这让我对我们为公司和客户塑造下一阶段增长的能力充满信心。接下来，让我把它交给艾米来介绍一下我们的财务业绩和前景。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Thank you, Satya. Good afternoon, everyone. We delivered results that exceeded expectations across revenue, operating income, and earnings per share, driven by strong demand and execution. As Satya shared, our AI business annual revenue run rate surpassed $37 billion this quarter, growing 123% year over year. And we are accelerating our pace of innovation as we execute against the expansive opportunity ahead. This quarter, revenue was $82.9 billion, up 1815% in constant currency. Gross margin dollars increased 1613% in constant currency, while operating income increased 2016% in constant currency. Earnings per share was $4.27, an increase of 218% in constant currency when adjusted for the impact from our investment in OpenAI.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：谢谢你，萨蒂亚。大家下午好。在强劲的需求和执行力的推动下，我们在收入、营业收入和每股收益方面取得了超出预期的业绩。正如 Satya 分享的那样，本季度我们的 AI 业务年收入运行率超过 370 亿美元，同比增长 123%。我们正在加快创新步伐，抓住未来广阔的机遇。本季度收入为 829 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 1815%。按固定汇率计算，毛利率增长 1613%，而营业收入按固定汇率计算，增长 2016%。每股收益为 4.27 美元，根据我们投资 OpenAI 的影响进行调整后，按固定汇率计算增长了 218%。

pairs.en: And FX was roughly in line with guidance at the total company level. Company gross margin percentage was 68%, down year over year, driven by continued investment in AI infrastructure and growing AI product usage. The impact from these investments was partially offset by ongoing efficiency gains, particularly in Azure and Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud. Operating expenses increased 98% in constant currency, driven by continued investment in AI, including R&D compute capacity, talent, and data to support product development across the portfolio. This quarter, growth was impacted by a low prior-year comparable, particularly in sales and marketing and G&A expenses. Operating margins increased slightly year over year, to 46%.
pairs.zh: 外汇市场与整个公司层面的指引大致一致。由于对人工智能基础设施的持续投资和人工智能产品使用量的不断增长，公司毛利率为68%，同比下降。这些投资的影响被持续的效率提升所部分抵消，特别是在 Azure 和 Microsoft 365 商业云方面。由于对人工智能的持续投资（包括支持整个产品组合的产品开发的研发计算能力、人才和数据），运营支出按固定汇率计算增长了 98%。本季度的增长受到上年同期较低的影响，特别是在销售和营销以及一般管理费用方面。营业利润率同比略有增长，达到 46%。

pairs.en: Total company headcount declined year over year as we focus on building high-performing teams that operate with pace and agility. When adjusted for the impact from our investments in OpenAI, other income and expense was $961 million. Favorability was driven by gains on investments that were partially offset by losses on foreign currency remeasurement. Capital expenditures were $31.9 billion, down due to the normal variability from cloud infrastructure buildouts and the timing of delivery of finance leases. And this quarter, roughly two-thirds of our CapEx was for short-lived assets, primarily GPUs and CPUs. The remaining spend was for long-lived assets that will support monetization over the next fifteen years and beyond.
pairs.zh: 由于我们专注于建设快速、敏捷的高绩效团队，公司总员工人数逐年下降。根据 OpenAI 投资的影响进行调整后，其他收入和支出为 9.61 亿美元。投资收益推动了投资收益，但投资收益被外汇重新计量损失部分抵消。由于云基础设施建设和融资租赁交付时间的正常变化，资本支出为 319 亿美元。本季度，我们大约三分之二的资本支出用于短期资产，主要是 GPU 和 CPU。剩余的支出用于长期资产，这些资产将支持未来十五年及以后的货币化。

pairs.en: This quarter, total finance leases were $4.7 billion and were primarily for large data center sites. Cash paid for PP&E was $30.9 billion, roughly in line with capital expenditures as the impact from finance leases was partially offset by differences between the receipt of goods and payment. Cash flow from operations was $46.7 billion, up 26% driven by strong cloud billings and collections, partially offset by an increase in operating lease payments. Free cash flow was $15.8 billion reflecting higher capital expenditures. And finally, we returned $10.2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Now to our commercial results. Commercial bookings grew 7% when excluding the impact from OpenAI, driven by consistent execution in our core annuity sales motions.
pairs.zh: 本季度融资租赁总额为 47 亿美元，主要针对大型数据中心站点。财产、厂房和设备支付的现金为 309 亿美元，大致与资本支出一致，因为融资租赁的影响被收货和付款之间的差异部分抵消。运营现金流为 467 亿美元，在强劲的云账单和收款推动下增长了 26%，但部分被经营租赁付款的增加所抵消。自由现金流为 158 亿美元，反映出资本支出增加。最后，我们通过股息和股票回购向股东返还 102 亿美元。现在来看看我们的商业成果。在我们核心年金销售行动的持续执行的推动下，排除 OpenAI 的影响，商业预订增长了 7%。

pairs.en: Bookings decreased 46% in constant currency when including Azure commitments from OpenAI. Commercial remaining performance obligation grew 26% in line with historic seasonality when excluding OpenAI. RPO increased to $627 billion and was up 99% year over year with a weighted average duration of approximately two and a half years when including OpenAI. Roughly 25% will be recognized in revenue in the next twelve months, up 39% year over year. The remaining portion recognized beyond the next twelve months increased 138%. Microsoft Cloud revenue was $54.5 billion and grew 2925% in constant currency, reflecting strong demand across the Azure platform and our first-party AI applications and services.
pairs.zh: 如果考虑 OpenAI 的 Azure 承诺，按固定汇率计算，预订量下降了 46%。不包括 OpenAI 的情况下，商业剩余履约义务增长了 26%，与历史季节性相符。RPO 增至 6270 亿美元，同比增长 99%，包括 OpenAI 在内的加权平均持续时间约为两年半。大约 25% 将在未来 12 个月内确认为收入，同比增长 39%。未来 12 个月后确认的剩余部分增加了 138%。Microsoft 云收入为 545 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 2925%，反映出 Azure 平台以及我们的第一方 AI 应用程序和服务的强劲需求。

pairs.en: Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage was slightly better than expected, at 66%, and down year over year due to continued investments in AI, partially offset by the ongoing efficiency gains noted earlier. Now to our segment results. Revenue from Productivity and Business Processes was $35 billion and grew 1713% in constant currency. Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud revenue increased 1915% in constant currency, ahead of expectations. Strong execution and improving product quality drove accelerating Microsoft 365 Copilot seat adds this quarter, with paid seats now over 20 million. ARPU growth was again led by both E5 and Microsoft 365 Copilot.
pairs.zh: 微软云毛利率略好于预期，为 66%，由于对人工智能的持续投资而同比下降，但部分被前面提到的持续效率提升所抵消。现在我们的细分结果。生产力和业务流程收入为 350 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 1713%。按固定汇率计算，Microsoft 365 商业云收入增长 1915%，超出预期。强大的执行力和不断提高的产品质量推动了本季度 Microsoft 365 Copilot 席位的加速增长，付费席位现已超过 2000 万。E5 和 Microsoft 365 Copilot 再次引领 ARPU 增长。

pairs.en: Paid Microsoft 365 Commercial seats grew 6% year over year, with installed base expansion across all customer segments, though primarily in our small and medium business and frontline worker offerings. Microsoft 365 Commercial products revenue increased 1% and decreased 3% in constant currency, down sequentially as Office 2024 transactional purchasing trends continued to normalize as expected. Microsoft 365 Consumer cloud revenue increased 3329% in constant currency, again driven by ARPU growth. Microsoft 365 Consumer subscriptions grew 7%. LinkedIn revenue increased 129% in constant currency with growth across all lines of business. Dynamics 365 revenue increased 2217% in constant currency with continued share gains and growth across all workloads.
pairs.zh: 付费 Microsoft 365 商业席位同比增长 6%，所有客户群体的安装基础都在扩大，但主要是在我们的中小型企业和一线员工产品中。按固定汇率计算，Microsoft 365 商业产品收入增长 1%，下降 3%，随着 Office 2024 交易购买趋势继续正常化，环比下降。按固定汇率计算，Microsoft 365 消费者云收入增长了 3329%，这再次受到 ARPU 增长的推动。Microsoft 365 消费者订阅量增长了 7%。按固定汇率计算，LinkedIn 收入增长了 129%，所有业务线均实现增长。按固定汇率计算，Dynamics 365 收入增长了 2217%，所有工作负载的份额持续增长和增长。

pairs.en: Bookings growth was impacted by weaker renewals, as customers balance spend between the traditional per-seat and the emerging seats-plus-consumption model. Segment gross margin dollars increased 1813% and gross margin percentage increased slightly, again driven by efficiency gains at Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud that were partially offset by continued investments in AI, including the impact of growing adoption and usage of Copilot. Against a low prior-year comparable, operating expenses increased 119% in constant currency driven by the shared R&D AI investments mentioned earlier, as well as higher Copilot advertising spend. Operating income increased 2114% in constant currency, and operating margins increased year over year to 60%. Next, the Intelligent Cloud segment. Revenue was $34.7 billion and grew 3028% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 预订增长受到续订疲弱的影响，因为客户在传统的每座位和新兴的座位加消费模式之间平衡支出。部门毛利率增长了 1813%，毛利率百分比略有上升，这再次受到 Microsoft 365 商业云效率提升的推动，但人工智能的持续投资（包括 Copilot 的采用和使用不断增长的影响）部分抵消了效率提升。与上年同期较低水平相比，由于前面提到的共享研发人工智能投资以及 Copilot 广告支出的增加，运营费用按固定汇率计算增长了 119%。按固定汇率计算，营业收入增长了 2114%，营业利润率同比增长至 60%。接下来是智能云部分。收入为 347 亿美元，按固定汇率计算增长 3028%。

pairs.en: In Azure and other cloud services, revenue grew 4039% in constant currency, against a prior year that included accelerating growth. Results were ahead of expectations as we delivered capacity earlier in the quarter, enabling increased consumption across both AI and non-AI services. Strong customer demand across workloads, customer segments, and geographic regions continues to exceed available capacity. In our on-premises server business, revenue increased slightly and decreased 3% in constant currency with ongoing customer shift to cloud offerings. Segment gross margin dollars increased 1918% in constant currency. Gross margin percentage decreased year over year driven by continued AI investment and increased GitHub Copilot usage, partially offset by ongoing efficiency gains in Azure.
pairs.zh: 在 Azure 和其他云服务中，按固定汇率计算，收入增长了 4039%，而上一年则加速增长。我们在本季度早些时候交付了容量，结果超出了预期，从而增加了人工智能和非人工智能服务的消费。跨工作负载、客户群和地理区域的强劲客户需求持续超出可用容量。在我们的本地服务器业务中，随着客户不断转向云产品，收入略有增长，但按固定汇率计算下降了 3%。按固定汇率计算，分部毛利率增长 1918%。由于持续的人工智能投资和 GitHub Copilot 使用量的增加，毛利率同比下降，但部分被 Azure 持续提高的效率所抵消。

pairs.en: Operating expenses increased 97% in constant currency, driven by the shared R&D AI investment noted earlier. Operating income grew 2423% in constant currency, and operating margins were 40%. Now to More Personal Computing. Revenue was $13.2 billion and declined 1% and 3% in constant currency. Windows OEM and devices revenue decreased 23% in constant currency. Windows OEM increased slightly and was ahead of expectations as OEM and channel partners continued to build inventory given increasing memory prices. Search advertising revenue ex-TAC increased 129% in constant currency with growth driven by higher volume and revenue per search across Edge and Bing. In gaming, revenue decreased 79% in constant currency.
pairs.zh: 在前面提到的共享研发人工智能投资的推动下，按固定汇率计算，运营费用增长了 97%。按固定汇率计算，营业收入增长 2423%，营业利润率为 40%。现在介绍更多个人计算。收入为 132 亿美元，按固定汇率计算分别下降 1% 和 3%。按固定汇率计算，Windows OEM 和设备收入下降了 23%。由于内存价格上涨，OEM 和渠道合作伙伴继续增加库存，Windows OEM 略有增长，并且超出预期。按固定汇率计算，不计 TAC 的搜索广告收入增长了 129%，增长的推动因素是 Edge 和 Bing 的搜索量和每次搜索收入的增加。在游戏领域，按固定汇率计算，收入下降了 79%。

pairs.en: Xbox content and services revenue decreased 57% in constant currency, against a prior comparable that benefited from strong first-party content performance. Segment gross margin dollars increased 64% in constant currency, and gross margin percentage increased year over year driven by a sales mix shift to higher-margin businesses. Against a low prior-year comparable, operating expenses increased 76% in constant currency driven by impairment and other related expenses in our gaming business, as well as continued investments in shared R&D mentioned earlier that benefits the entire portfolio. Operating income increased 41% in constant currency, and operating margins increased year over year to 28%. Now moving to our Q4 outlook, which, unless specifically noted otherwise, is on a US dollar basis.
pairs.zh: 按固定汇率计算，Xbox 内容和服务收入下降了 57%，而之前的可比业绩得益于第一方内容的强劲表现。按固定汇率计算，该部门的毛利率增长了 64%，由于销售组合转向利润率较高的业务，毛利率百分比同比增长。与上年同期较低水平相比，由于我们游戏业务的减值和其他相关费用，以及前面提到的共享研发的持续投资，按固定汇率计算，运营费用增长了 76%，这有利于整个投资组合。按固定汇率计算，营业收入增长 41%，营业利润率同比增长至 28%。现在转向我们的第四季度展望，除非另有特别说明，否则都是以美元为基础的。

pairs.en: Based on current rates, we expect FX to increase revenue growth by roughly one point in Productivity and Business Processes and More Personal Computing, with no meaningful impact to Intelligent Cloud. Overall impact to total revenue is expected to be less than one point. FX should increase COGS growth by roughly one point with no impact to operating expense growth. Starting with our commercial business, in commercial bookings, when adjusted for the impact from OpenAI, we expect healthy growth on a growing expiry base with consistent execution in our core annuity sales motions against a significant prior-year comparable.
pairs.zh: 根据目前的增长率，我们预计 FX 将在生产力、业务流程以及更多个人计算方面将收入增长提高大约一个百分点，而对智能云不会产生有意义的影响。预计对总收入的总体影响不到 1 个百分点。外汇应该会使销货成本增长大约一个百分点，而对运营费用增长没有影响。从我们的商业业务开始，在商业预订方面，当根据 OpenAI 的影响进行调整时，我们预计在不断增长的到期基础上实现健康增长，并与上一年的重大可比性相比，我们的核心年金销售行动的一致执行。

pairs.en: Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage should be roughly 64%, down year over year driven by continued investments in AI and increased GitHub Copilot usage. Just this week, we announced a business model transition in GitHub Copilot that will align pricing with usage and value that takes effect on June 1. Now to segment guidance. In Productivity and Business Processes, we expect revenue of $37.0 to $37.3 billion or growth of 12% to 13%. In Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud on an adjusted basis, we expect revenue growth to be between 15 and 16 in constant currency, when normalized for the prior-year comparable that benefited from two points of in-period revenue recognition.
pairs.zh: 由于对人工智能的持续投资和 GitHub Copilot 使用量的增加，微软云毛利率应约为 64%，同比下降。就在本周，我们宣布了 GitHub Copilot 的业务模式转变，该转变将使定价与使用情况和价值保持一致，并于 6 月 1 日生效。现在来细分指导。在生产力和业务流程方面，我们预计收入为 37.0 至 373 亿美元，或增长 12% 至 13%。在调整后的 Microsoft 365 商业云中，我们预计按固定汇率计算的收入增长将在 15 至 16 之间，对受益于两个期间收入确认点的上年可比进行标准化。

pairs.en: On a reported basis, we expect revenue growth to be between 13 and 14% in constant currency. Building on the Copilot momentum we saw in Q3, we expect net paid seat adds to increase sequentially, which will drive continued ARPU growth. Microsoft 365 Commercial products revenue should grow in the mid-single digits against a prior year that benefited from higher-than-expected Office 2024 transactional purchasing. As a reminder, Microsoft 365 Commercial products includes components that can be variable due to in-period revenue recognition dynamics. Microsoft 365 Consumer cloud revenue growth should be in the low 20% range, down sequentially as we start to lap the benefit from last year's price increase.
pairs.zh: 根据报告，我们预计按固定汇率计算收入增长将在 13% 至 14% 之间。基于我们在第三季度看到的 Copilot 势头，我们预计净付费座位数将连续增加，这将推动 ARPU 持续增长。受益于高于预期的 Office 2024 交易购买，Microsoft 365 商业产品收入应比上年增长中个位数。提醒一下，Microsoft 365 商业产品包含的组件可能会因期内收入确认动态而变化。Microsoft 365 消费者云收入增长应该在 20% 的低水平范围内，随着我们开始享受去年价格上涨的好处，环比下降。

pairs.en: Growth will again be driven by ARPU and an increase in subscription volume. For LinkedIn, we expect revenue growth of approximately 10%. In Dynamics 365, we expect revenue growth to be in the low double digits, down sequentially with impact from a strong prior-year comparable and the bookings trends noted earlier. For Intelligent Cloud, we expect revenue of $37.95 to $38.25 billion or growth of 27% to 28%. In Azure, we continue to focus on accelerating the delivery of capacity and increasing fleet efficiencies. Therefore, we expect Q4 revenue growth to be between 39 and 40% in constant currency against a strong prior-year comparable that included accelerating growth.
pairs.zh: 增长将再次受到 ARPU 和订阅量增加的推动。对于 LinkedIn，我们预计收入增长约为 10%。在 Dynamics 365 中，我们预计收入增长将处于较低的两位数，由于去年强劲的可比性和前面提到的预订趋势的影响，收入将连续下降。对于智能云，我们预计收入为 37.95 美元至 382.5 亿美元，增长 27% 至 28%。在 Azure 中，我们继续专注于加速容量交付和提高车队效率。因此，我们预计第四季度收入增长（按固定汇率计算）将在 39% 至 40% 之间，而上年同期收入增长强劲，其中包括加速增长。

pairs.en: Broad and growing customer demand continues to exceed supply, and we continue to balance the incoming supply we can allocate here against our other high-ROI priorities: first-party applications, R&D, and end-of-life server replacement. As a reminder, year-over-year Azure growth rates can vary quarter to quarter, based on capacity, timing, and contract mix. In our on-premise server business, we expect revenue to decline in the mid-single digits with ongoing customer shift to cloud offerings. In More Personal Computing, we are lapping strong prior-year comparables, navigating complex PC market dynamics impacted by memory prices, and refocusing on delivering quality and value to consumers. Therefore, we expect revenue to be $11.75 to $12.25 billion.
pairs.zh: 广泛且不断增长的客户需求继续超过供应，我们继续平衡我们可以在这里分配的传入供应与其他高投资回报率优先事项：第一方应用程序、研发和报废服务器更换。提醒一下，Azure 的同比增长率可能会因容量、时间安排和合同组合而有所不同。在我们的本地服务器业务中，随着客户不断转向云产品，我们预计收入将出现中个位数下降。在更多个人计算方面，我们正在追赶上年的强劲可比性，应对受内存价格影响的复杂个人电脑市场动态，并重新专注于为消费者提供质量和价值。因此，我们预计收入为 11.75 至 122.5 亿美元。

pairs.en: Windows OEM revenue should decline in the high teens with roughly six points of impact from a prior-year comparable that benefited from Windows 10 end of support, six points from inventory levels that we expect to come down for the quarter, and six points from a lower PC market as prices increase due to memory cost. The range of potential outcomes remains wider than normal. Therefore, Windows OEM and devices revenue should decline in the mid- to high teens. Search advertising revenue ex-TAC growth should be in the high single digits driven by revenue per search and volume with continued share gains across Bing and Edge.
pairs.zh: Windows OEM 收入应该会下降十多岁，与去年同期相比，受益于 Windows 10 终止支持，大约会受到 6 个百分点的影响；我们预计本季度的库存水平会下降 6 个百分点；由于内存成本导致价格上涨，PC 市场的收入会下降 6 个百分点。潜在结果的范围仍然比正常情况更广泛。因此，Windows OEM 和设备收入应该会下降到十几位数。不计 TAC 的搜索广告收入增长应该会保持在较高的个位数增长，这是由每次搜索收入和数量以及 Bing 和 Edge 的份额持续增长所推动的。

pairs.en: In Xbox content and services, we expect revenue to decline in the low teens, reflecting a prior-year comparable that benefited from strong first-party content as well as the recent price changes for Xbox Game Pass as we focus on delivering more value to gamers. Hardware revenue should decline year over year. Therefore, at the total company level, revenue should be between $86.7 and $87.8 billion or growth of 13% to 15%, with accelerating commercial growth partially offset by our consumer business. Our Q4 outlook for COGS and operating expenses includes roughly $900 million in one-time cost for the recently announced voluntary retirement program.
pairs.zh: 在 Xbox 内容和服务方面，我们预计收入将下降到十几岁以下，这反映出去年同期的收入得益于强劲的第一方内容以及最近 Xbox Game Pass 的价格变化，因为我们专注于为游戏玩家提供更多价值。硬件收入应该会逐年下降。因此，在整个公司层面，收入应在 86.7 至 878 亿美元之间，即增长 13% 至 15%，商业增长的加速被我们的消费者业务部分抵消。我们对第四季度销货成本和运营费用的展望包括最近宣布的自愿退休计划的一次性成本约 9 亿美元。

pairs.en: Therefore, we expect COGS of $29.4 to $29.6 billion or growth of 22% to 23%, including roughly $350 million from the retirement program, and operating expense of $19.3 to $19.4 billion or growth of approximately 7%, including roughly $550 million from the retirement program. Even as we invested through the year in additional capacity to serve the growing AI platform, apps, and services demand, and inclusive of these one-time costs, we expect full-year FY '26 operating margins to be up about one point year over year.
pairs.zh: 因此，我们预计销货成本为 29.4 至 296 亿美元，或增长 22% 至 23%，其中包括来自退休计划的约 3.5 亿美元，运营费用为 19.3 至 194 亿美元，或增长约 7%，包括来自退休计划的约 5.5 亿美元。尽管我们全年投资额外产能以满足不断增长的人工智能平台、应用程序和服务需求，并包含这些一次性成本，但我们预计 26 财年全年运营利润率仍将同比增长约一个百分点。

pairs.en: Excluding any impact from our investments in OpenAI, other income and expense is expected to be roughly negative $100 million as interest income will be more than offset by interest expense, which includes the interest payments related to data center finance leases. We expect our adjusted Q4 effective tax rate to be approximately 19%. Next, capital expenditures. We expect CapEx spend to increase to over $40 billion as we continue to bring more capacity online. The sequential increase includes roughly $5 billion from higher component pricing as well as the impact from finance leases, which add variability given full value is recorded in the period of lease commencement.
pairs.zh: 排除我们对 OpenAI 投资的影响，其他收入和支出预计约为负 1 亿美元，因为利息收入将被利息支出（包括与数据中心融资租赁相关的利息支付）所抵消。我们预计调整后的第四季度有效税率约为 19%。接下来是资本支出。随着我们继续增加在线容量，我们预计资本支出将增加到 400 亿美元以上。环比增长包括约 50 亿美元的组件定价上涨以及融资租赁的影响，考虑到租赁开始期间记录的全部价值，这增加了可变性。

pairs.en: For calendar year 2026, we expect the mix of short-lived assets to remain similar to Q3. We expect to invest roughly $190 billion in capital expenditures, which includes approximately $25 billion from the impact of higher component pricing. We remain confident in the return on these investments given higher demand signals and increasing product usage, as well as the efficiencies we are already driving across the platform. Even with these additional investments and continued efforts to bring GPU, CPU, and storage capacity online faster, we expect to remain constrained at least through 2026.
pairs.zh: 对于 2026 年，我们预计短期资产的结构将与第三季度保持相似。我们预计将投资约 1,900 亿美元的资本支出，其中包括约 250 亿美元来自组件定价上涨的影响。鉴于更高的需求信号和不断增加的产品使用量，以及我们已经在整个平台上提高的效率，我们对这些投资的回报仍然充满信心。即使进行了这些额外投资并持续努力使 GPU、CPU 和存储容量更快上线，我们预计至少到 2026 年仍将受到限制。

pairs.en: Despite these constraints, and the continued need to balance incoming supply, we expect Azure growth to show modest acceleration in the second half of the calendar year, compared with the first half. Now I would like to share some closing thoughts as we look to next fiscal year. First, we continue to evolve how we operate to increase our pace and agility. Therefore, we expect headcount will decrease year over year. Operating expense growth will be in the mid- to high single digits, reflecting ongoing investments in R&D, inclusive of AI investment in compute, data, and talent to accelerate product innovation.
pairs.zh: 尽管存在这些限制，并且仍然需要平衡传入的供应，但我们预计与上半年相比，Azure 的增长在下半年将显示出适度的加速。现在，当我们展望下一个财年时，我想分享一些结束语。首先，我们不断改进我们的运营方式，以提高我们的速度和敏捷性。因此，我们预计员工人数将逐年减少。运营费用增长将达到中高个位数，反映出对研发的持续投资，包括对计算、数据和人才的人工智能投资，以加速产品创新。

pairs.en: Next, as a reminder, we will lap strong prior-year comparables impacted by Windows 10 end of support, elevated OEM inventory levels, as well as increased Office and server transactional purchasing. Finally, we remain focused on delivering a platform that enables customers to build and run AI solutions and on driving innovation in our first-party AI applications and services. Therefore, we expect another year of double-digit revenue and operating income growth in FY '27. In closing, we are committed to delivering innovation that helps customers create new business value as we enter the final quarter of our fiscal year. With that, let us go to Q&A, Jonathan.
pairs.zh: 接下来，提醒一下，我们将受到 Windows 10 支持终止、OEM 库存水平上升以及 Office 和服务器交易采购增加的影响，实现强劲的上年可比性。最后，我们仍然专注于提供一个平台，使客户能够构建和运行人工智能解决方案，并推动我们的第一方人工智能应用程序和服务的创新。因此，我们预计 27 财年的收入和营业收入将再次实现两位数增长。最后，我们致力于在进入财年最后一个季度时提供创新，帮助客户创造新的业务价值。接下来，让我们进入问答环节，乔纳森。

pairs.en: Jonathan Neilson:Thanks, Amy. We will now open the call for questions. Out of respect for others on the call, we request that participants please only ask one question. Operator, can you please repeat your instructions?
pairs.zh: 乔纳森·尼尔森：谢谢，艾米。我们现在开始提问。出于对通话中其他人的尊重，我们要求参与者仅提出一个问题。接线员，您能重复一下您的指示吗？

pairs.en: Operator:And our first question comes from the line of Keith Weiss with Morgan Stanley. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 接线员：我们的第一个问题来自摩根士丹利的 Keith Weiss 的线路。请继续。

pairs.en: Keith Weiss:Excellent. Thank you for taking the question, and congratulations on another really solid quarter. Those Microsoft 365 Copilot numbers are super impressive and way ahead of most people's expectations. I wanted to ask a broader question on demand. We have been talking about strong demand for a while. We see it in our CIO surveys, and you definitely express it in what you are seeing in your business. Maybe in the short term, can you talk to us about how that demand translates into commercial bookings and how that might be changing? You mentioned different contracting cycles between seats and consumption that may impact that, and then we also have to think about renewal bases.
pairs.zh: 基思·韦斯：非常好。感谢您提出问题，并祝贺您又一个非常出色的季度。这些 Microsoft 365 Copilot 数据非常令人印象深刻，远远超出了大多数人的预期。我想根据需要问一个更广泛的问题。我们谈论强劲需求已经有一段时间了。我们在 CIO 调查中看到了这一点，而您在业务中所看到的情况中也肯定表达了这一点。也许在短期内，您能和我们谈谈这种需求如何转化为商业预订以及这种情况可能会发生怎样的变化吗？您提到了席位和消费之间不同的合同周期可能会影响这一点，然后我们还必须考虑续订基础。

pairs.en: Longer term, and maybe this opens it up to Satya, what is supporting this demand over time? Or said another way, who is paying for all this? Because while we see excitement for Microsoft in our CIO survey, our overall IT expectations are not increasing, and GDP growth is not really increasing. So at some point, how does this get paid for, and do you start to see the indications of where those dollars are going to come from? Thank you.
pairs.zh: 从长远来看，也许这向萨蒂亚敞开了大门，随着时间的推移，是什么支撑着这种需求？或者换句话说，谁为这一切买单？因为虽然我们在 CIO 调查中看到了对 Microsoft 的兴奋，但我们的整体 IT 预期并没有增加，GDP 增长也没有真正增加。那么在某个时候，这是如何支付的，你是否开始看到这些美元将来自哪里的迹象？谢谢。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Why do I not start with the first half of your question, Keith, around how some of these models impact bookings? I think it is really important. You are right. We have the normal cyclical things that happen with bookings. It is the expiration base, or large multiyear Azure commitments that get signed, and that has always had some volatility to it. But if you take a step back, which is the broader question you are asking, you are really thinking through how we go from using a model that is historically thought of as a per-seat business to getting work done and being more productive as a seat or a worker plus an agent.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：基思，我为什么不从你问题的前半部分开始，了解其中一些模型如何影响预订？我认为这非常重要。你是对的。我们有预订时发生的正常周期性事情。它是到期基础，或者是签署的大型多年期 Azure 承诺，并且始终存在一些波动性。但如果你退后一步，这是你所问的更广泛的问题，你会真正思考我们如何从使用历史上被认为是按席位业务的模式转变为完成工作并作为席位或工人加代理提高工作效率。

pairs.en: When I think about that model, I start to think about it as a licensed business plus a consumption business applied far more broadly than I think people have thought about. It starts to mean that over time, bookings will actually also look a little different. It will still have that per-seat license logic, but it will also have a meter, just like you see in Azure. It may not all flow through bookings in the same way; you will just bill for usage. If that usage has great value to customers—then you will keep spinning and keep using those agents if they are adding direct value or growth to your business.
pairs.zh: 当我思考这种模式时，我开始将其视为许可业务加上消费业务，其应用范围比我认为人们想象的要广泛得多。这意味着随着时间的推移，预订实际上也会有所不同。它仍然具有每个席位的许可证逻辑，但它也将具有一个仪表，就像您在 Azure 中看到的那样。预订流程可能并非全部都以相同的方式进行；您只需按使用量计费。如果这种使用对客户具有巨大价值，那么您将继续旋转并继续使用这些代理（如果它们为您的业务增加直接价值或增长）。

pairs.en: I think it is healthy to start to think about that transition in a broader way. While you may not see it in the short term in bookings, if I were to frame how to think about the opportunity, I would think about it more in that light.
pairs.zh: 我认为开始以更广泛的方式思考这种转变是健康的。虽然你可能在短期内不会在预订中看到它，但如果我要框架如何思考这个机会，我会更多地从这个角度来思考。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Amy captured it. The basic transformation of any per-user business of ours—whether it is productivity, coding, or security—will become a per-user and usage business. That is the best way to think about it. It is already happening with coding, where you see it at scale. Some of the business model changes we made this quarter speak to that and also speak to the intensity of usage. Where are these dollars going to come from? At the end of the day, they will come from some eval and outcome that a business has where these agents—working on behalf of users or with users—have created value.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：艾米捕捉到了它。我们任何每用户业务的基本转变——无论是生产力、编码还是安全——都将变成每用户和使用业务。这是最好的思考方式。这种情况已经在编码中发生，你可以看到它的规模。我们本季度做出的一些业务模式变化说明了这一点，也说明了使用的强度。这些美元从哪里来？最终，它们将来自企业的一些评估和结果，这些代理（代表用户或与用户一起工作）创造了价值。

pairs.en: That is where it starts, whether it is customer service, individual productivity, team productivity, or a business process. Some cost is either decreasing because of the use of agents, or some revenue is increasing because agents compressed workflows. That is what you broadly start to see. Even when people talk about Copilot, they use chat and chat with reasoning, they use CoWork, they use agent mode inside Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, but it is all done in the context of some task trajectory. When they see that the task trajectory is compressing the workflow, improving revenue, or decreasing cost, that is what is driving usage. It may not be pure seat coverage motions like in the past.
pairs.zh: 无论是客户服务、个人生产力、团队生产力还是业务流程，这都是一切的起点。一些成本要么因为代理的使用而降低，要么因为代理压缩工作流程而增加一些收入。这就是你开始普遍看到的。即使人们谈论Copilot时，他们使用聊天和推理聊天，他们使用CoWork，他们在Word、Excel和PowerPoint中使用代理模式，但这都是在某些任务轨迹的背景下完成的。当他们看到任务轨迹正在压缩工作流程、提高收入或降低成本时，这就是推动使用的原因。这可能不是像过去那样纯粹的座位覆盖动议。

pairs.en: This is more about getting intense users and intense usage, and that is what we are focused on.
pairs.zh: 这更多的是为了获得大量的用户和大量的使用，这也是我们所关注的。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Keith, maybe just to take a quick second—just a big thank you to you. It has been a real privilege to work with you over many, many quarters. We have really appreciated your coverage over this time, and congratulations on your next chapter. Thank you so much.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：基思，也许只是想稍微休息一下——非常感谢你。能够与您合作很多很多个季度，我感到非常荣幸。我们非常感谢您对这段时间的报道，并祝贺您的​​下一章。太感谢了。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Keith, it has just been fantastic with you.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：基思，和你在一起真是太棒了。

pairs.en: Keith Weiss:I really appreciate that. Thank you so much.
pairs.zh: 基思·韦斯：我真的很感激。太感谢了。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Thank you. Operator, next question, please.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：谢谢。接线员，请下一个问题。

pairs.en: Operator:The next question comes from the line of Karl Keirstead with UBS. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 接线员：下一个问题来自瑞银集团的Karl Keirstead。请继续。

pairs.en: Karl Keirstead:Okay, great. Thank you. Maybe, Amy, could you elaborate a little bit on the CapEx guidance you just provided? Obviously, it requires a fairly material pickup in CapEx in the second half of the calendar year, maybe to the tune of $120 billion. I am just curious about your confidence in working through the physical component constraints to hit that number. Does it involve greater use of partners? And how are you thinking about allocating that increased capacity between third party and first party? Do you have a general framework you would advise us to keep in mind? Thank you.
pairs.zh: 卡尔·凯斯特德：好的，太好了。谢谢。艾米，您能否详细说明一下您刚刚提供的资本支出指南？显然，这需要在今年下半年大幅增加资本支出，可能达到 1200 亿美元。我只是好奇您是否有信心克服物理组件的限制来达到这个数字。它是否涉及更多地利用合作伙伴？您如何考虑在第三方和第一方之间分配增加的容量？您有建议我们牢记的总体框架吗？谢谢。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Sure. Thanks, Karl. I actually feel quite good about our ability to work through the physical limitations. I think of the industrial logic of the supply chain to be able to put that in place. Some of that, as we have talked about, is getting capacity online, but a lot of that is short term in nature—being able to get CPUs, GPUs, and storage put in place to better support the demand signals we have been seeing. We tried to give some help on part of that being price; I think that just helps give you a sense on volumes, and obviously it leans more to short-term assets when you see that type of impact of price on the number.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：当然。谢谢，卡尔。事实上，我对我们克服身体限制的能力感到非常满意。我认为供应链的工业逻辑能够将其落实到位。正如我们所讨论的，其中一些是获得在线容量，但其中很多本质上都是短期的——能够将 CPU、GPU 和存储部署到位，以更好地支持我们所看到的需求信号。我们试图在价格方面提供一些帮助；我认为这有助于让您了解交易量，而且当您看到价格对数量的这种影响时，显然它更倾向于短期资产。

pairs.en: In terms of allocation, you should assume—based on what you were seeing in Azure—looking for 39 to 40 in constant currency in Q4 means that we are able to use some to make sure we are able to meet demand as we can, and do that in a balanced way across Azure. Our Copilot usage in Q3 has really been on a different trajectory than we saw up to this point. That applies across coding, across productivity, and I have some confidence it will also apply across security.
pairs.zh: 在分配方面，您应该假设（基于您在 Azure 中看到的情况）在第四季度寻找 39 到 40 个固定货币意味着我们能够使用一些来确保我们能够尽可能满足需求，并在整个 Azure 中以平衡的方式做到这一点。我们在第三季度的副驾驶使用情况确实与我们迄今为止所看到的不同。这适用于编码、生产力，而且我有信心它也适用于安全性。

pairs.en: When we talk about some acceleration into what I would call the first half of FY '27—the second half of the calendar year—it means we are getting insights into our abilities to increasingly put pressure on efficiencies, speed up the deliveries into our data centers, and make that revenue-ready as quickly as we can. I would expect the pressure between first-party usage and being able to meet Azure demand will persist, as I said, but we are doing our best to get things in as quickly as we can—hence the CapEx number that we see in the second half of the year.
pairs.zh: 当我们谈论我所说的 27 财年上半年（日历年下半年）的一些加速时，这意味着我们正在深入了解我们的能力，以加大对效率的压力，加快向我们的数据中心的交付，并尽快为收入做好准备。正如我所说，我预计第一方使用和满足 Azure 需求之间的压力将持续存在，但我们正在尽最大努力尽快完成工作，因此我们在今年下半年看到了资本支出数字。

pairs.en: Karl Keirstead:Okay. Terrific. Thank you.
pairs.zh: 卡尔·凯斯特德：好的。了不起。谢谢。

pairs.en: Jonathan Neilson:Thanks, Karl. Operator, next question, please.
pairs.zh: 乔纳森·尼尔森：谢谢，卡尔。接线员，请下一个问题。

pairs.en: Operator:The next question comes from the line of Brent Thill with Jefferies. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 接线员：下一个问题来自 Brent Thill 和 Jefferies 的线路。请继续。

pairs.en: Brent Thill:Thanks, Amy. One of the big pushbacks we all get is that AI is going to be really expensive, yet you, Google, and Amazon are showing higher margins tonight as you report. What are investors missing, and why is AI a potential better margin for the industry over time?
pairs.zh: 布伦特·希尔：谢谢，艾米。我们所有人都遇到的最大阻力之一是人工智能将非常昂贵，但正如您所报告的那样，您、谷歌和亚马逊今晚的利润率却更高。投资者错过了什么？为什么随着时间的推移，人工智能可能为该行业带来更好的利润？

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Thanks, Brent. We have been talking about where this AI business of ours has been in the cycle compared to the cycle we saw with the cloud, which now seems very long ago, and how margins were actually better and have remained better in our AI business versus where we saw them in the cloud transition looking back. We have been really focused on making sure that the business models reflect how these applications are both getting built and the value that they are bringing. When you think about that type of value, it tends to be captured more in consumption and usage-based pricing models, and I think that has been a little underappreciated in terms of margins going forward.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：谢谢，布伦特。我们一直在讨论，与我们在云计算中看到的周期相比，我们的人工智能业务处于周期的哪个阶段，现在看来，这似乎是很久以前的事了，以及我们的人工智能业务的利润率实际上比我们在云转型中看到的更好，并且一直保持得更好。我们一直致力于确保业务模型能够反映这些应用程序的构建方式以及它们所带来的价值。当你考虑这种类型的价值时，它往往更多地体现在基于消费和基于使用的定价模型中，而且我认为就未来的利润率而言，这一点被低估了。

pairs.en: It has been important to make sure we leverage the IP we have. The IP we get from our partnerships is obviously free to us for a long time, so we are able to take that and apply it to benefit our margins in a healthy way. You have also seen us work hard on the first-party hardware stack—being able to take cost out of the infra stack as well. And then, of course, the efficiency work. We have been in an accelerated phase of trying to get as much capacity as we can into production.
pairs.zh: 确保我们利用我们拥有的知识产权非常重要。我们从合作伙伴关系中获得的知识产权显然对我们来说是长期免费的，因此我们能够利用它并以健康的方式应用它来提高我们的利润。您还看到我们在第一方硬件堆栈上努力工作，也能够降低基础设施堆栈的成本。当然，还有效率工作。我们一直处于加速阶段，试图将尽可能多的产能投入生产。

pairs.en: When you go through that, you also then start to focus on the efficiency work—on the hardware side as well as on the software side—to be able to deliver these types of margins. One of the real focuses we all have to have—and this dates back to Keith’s question—is that when you move to usage-based models, you have to make sure you are delivering incredibly high value to customers. The focus starts with customer usage that creates value. If that creates value and positive output, then the TAM expansion here and the ROI will be very good.
pairs.zh: 当您经历这些时，您也会开始关注硬件方面和软件方面的效率工作，以便能够提供这些类型的利润。我们所有人都必须关注的真正焦点之一（这可以追溯到基思的问题）是，当您转向基于使用的模型时，您必须确保为客户提供令人难以置信的高价值。重点从创造价值的客户使用开始。如果这创造了价值和积极的产出，那么这里的 TAM 扩张和投资回报率将非常好。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Thanks, Brent. Operator, next question, please.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：谢谢，布伦特。接线员，请下一个问题。

pairs.en: Operator:The next question comes from the line of Mark Moerdler with Bernstein Research. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 操作员：下一个问题来自伯恩斯坦研究公司的马克·莫尔德勒。请继续。

pairs.en: Mark Moerdler:Thank you very much for taking my question, and congratulations on the quarter you delivered and the rate of growth and some of the commentary you have made on guidance. I would like to drill in a little bit on the CapEx and the spending that you are making. Obviously, the commercial cloud is growing fast, Azure is growing fast, and AI is growing even faster within your overall business. But there is a bit of a disconnect that makes investors a bit nervous between how fast they are seeing CapEx growing and how fast they are seeing revenue growing.
pairs.zh: 马克·莫尔德勒：非常感谢您提出我的问题，并对您交付的季度和增长率以及您对指导所做的一些评论表示祝贺。我想深入了解一下您的资本支出和支出。显然，商业云正在快速增长，Azure 正在快速增长，而人工智能在您的整体业务中增长得更快。但资本支出增长速度与收入增长速度之间存在一些脱节，让投资者感到有点紧张。

pairs.en: Can you give some color about how the timing works out, or how much needs to be spent on replacement of equipment or first party, in order to build confidence that as we look toward this strong spending on CapEx, the core business will continue to be very healthy and that the margins will be good? Thank you.
pairs.zh: 您能否详细说明一下时间安排，或者需要花多少钱来更换设备或第一方，以便建立信心，相信当我们期待资本支出的强劲支出时，核心业务将继续非常健康，利润率也会很高？谢谢。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Thanks, Mark. Let me start with Azure, which—given its size and its growth rates—where we have talked about acceleration from where we are, is the guide at 39 to 40 into a bigger number in the second half of the calendar year. When you start to see that type of growth rate on the size of the business we have, the amount of spend being done on short-term assets—which is the thing that correlates with revenue, as opposed to the third of that number that is going into fifteen-year assets, or some lumpy timing from lease contracts that can get confusing—I think in many ways this reminds us of the last cycle.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：谢谢，马克。让我从 Azure 开始，考虑到它的规模和增长率，我们已经讨论过从目前的位置加速，它是下半年 39 到 40 的一个更大数字的指南。当你开始看到我们的业务规模的增长率、短期资产的支出金额（与收入相关的东西，而不是十五年资产的支出的三分之一，或者租赁合同中可能令人困惑的一些不稳定的时间安排）时，我认为这在很多方面让我们想起了上一个周期。

pairs.en: When the TAM is so expansive and when shortages are generally growing between supply and demand, it gives you a lot of confidence in the ROI—certainly starting with the platform side. What you are really asking is whether, as we see these usage-plus-consumption models emerge at the app and services layer, are we starting to see the benefits of that? If you look at the last quarter, we saw some acceleration in the Microsoft 365 Commercial cloud number this quarter. We are guiding for that to be better again in Q4.
pairs.zh: 当 TAM 如此广泛且供需之间的短缺普遍加剧时，它会让您对投资回报率充满信心 - 当然从平台方面开始。您真正要问的是，当我们看到这些使用加消费模型出现在应用程序和服务层时，我们是否开始看到其好处？如果你看看上个季度，我们会发现本季度 Microsoft 365 商业云数量有所增长。我们预计第四季度情况将再次变得更好。

pairs.en: I think that is where you are starting to see what investors have been asking about—when we will start to see that show up in revenue growth—and that is the first place I would point to. We can also point to it in GitHub, where you see revenue growth rates and usage consumption models result in acceleration in the top line. In general, we continue to see that.
pairs.zh: 我认为这就是你开始看到投资者一直在问什么的地方——当我们开始看到收入增长时——这是我要指出的第一个地方。我们还可以在 GitHub 中指出这一点，您可以在其中看到收入增长率和使用消费模型导致收入加速增长。总的来说，我们继续看到这一点。

pairs.en: When you think about spending that amount of capital, putting it into production, seeing some delay before it turns into revenue-ready, having the book of business—over $600 billion of revenue that we still need to deliver—and that is before we are starting to see the acceleration in seats that we are seeing on Copilot, I feel very good about that number. Our real focus will be how much of that we can pull in as fast as we can.
pairs.zh: 当你考虑花费这么多资金，将其投入生产，在实现收入准备之前看到一些延迟，拥有业务账簿（我们仍然需要交付超过 6000 亿美元的收入），而这是在我们开始看到我们在 Copilot 上看到的座位数加速增长之前，我对这个数字感到非常满意。我们真正关注的是我们能尽快吸收多少。

pairs.en: I want to be transparent: when you have revenue sitting there that can be grown faster, or efficiencies to gain, the focus needs to be on doing that—landing this CapEx as quickly as we can and converting to revenue as quickly as we can.
pairs.zh: 我想坦白说：当你的收入可以更快增长，或者可以提高效率时，重点就应该放在这样做上——尽快实现资本支出，并尽快转化为收入。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:I will add one point. One of the things we have learned in the last two years in AI—and what builds more conviction and confidence—is where the TAM is and the category economics of the TAM. It is fascinating that here we are in 2026, and the most exciting things are plug-ins in Word or Excel, or CLIs in coding. When you see that, it means we have a structural position in knowledge work, coding, and security—which are the big TAMs. Then you couple that with the right business model—user plus usage—that Amy referenced multiple times. Even the book of business we have is a true line.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：我补充一点。过去两年我们在人工智能领域学到的一件事——以及建立更多信念和信心的东西——是 TAM 的位置以及 TAM 的类别经济学。令人着迷的是，我们已经到了 2026 年，最令人兴奋的事情是 Word 或 Excel 中的插件，或者编码中的 CLI。当你看到这一点时，这意味着我们在知识工作、编码和安全方面拥有结构性地位——这些都是重要的 TAM。然后，将其与艾米多次引用的正确的业务模型（用户加使用）结合起来。甚至我们的商业书籍也是一条真实的路线。

pairs.en: If anything, we want to make sure we are getting the CapEx to get the capacity in time for those increases in usage, which is going to be very key. The model capabilities are exponential. Think about agent mode in Excel—it kind of did not work until it started working, and that is because the model showed up. You have to be ready for those opportunities.
pairs.zh: 如果有的话，我们希望确保我们能够获得资本支出，以便及时获得容量以应对使用量的增加，这将是非常关键的。模型的能力呈指数级增长。想想 Excel 中的代理模式，它在开始工作之前就不起作用，这是因为模型出现了。您必须为这些机会做好准备。

pairs.en: Mark Moerdler:That is extremely helpful. I really do appreciate it, and again, congratulations on the quarter.
pairs.zh: 马克·莫德勒：这非常有帮助。我真的很感激，并再次祝贺这个季度。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Thanks, Mark. Operator, next question, please.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：谢谢，马克。接线员，请下一个问题。

pairs.en: Operator:The next question comes from the line of Gabriela Borges with Goldman Sachs. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 接线员：下一个问题来自高盛的加布里埃拉·博尔赫斯 (Gabriela Borges)。请继续。

pairs.en: Gabriela Borges:Hi, good afternoon. Thank you. Satya, I would love to hear some of your reflections on Copilot given the technical and commercial milestones that Microsoft has hit just in the last three months. Maybe share with us a little on your learnings from Copilot adoption to date—what you think is working, what is not working, and how that is informing your E7 strategy and the Copilot CoWork strategy. Thanks so much.
pairs.zh: 加布里埃拉·博尔赫斯：嗨，下午好。谢谢。Satya，考虑到 Microsoft 在过去三个月中取得的技术和商业里程碑，我很想听听您对 Copilot 的一些看法。也许可以与我们分享一些您迄今为止从 Copilot 采用过程中学到的经验教训 - 您认为什么有效，什么无效，以及这如何影响您的 E7 策略和 Copilot CoWork 策略。非常感谢。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Thank you for the question. The way to think about Copilot—especially Microsoft 365 Copilot in knowledge work—is that we have learned a lot from coding, but focusing on Microsoft 365 Copilot, the first thing is the form factor and the shape of the product and how it has evolved. There is chat—chat now with reasoning over WorkIQ. Then there are all the agents like Researcher and Analyst that you use within chat, or even custom agents that our customers are building. On top of that, you now have edit mode.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：谢谢你的提问。思考 Copilot（尤其是知识工作中的 Microsoft 365 Copilot）的方式是，我们从编码中学到了很多东西，但关注 Microsoft 365 Copilot，首先是产品的外形因素和形状以及它是如何演变的。有聊天功能——现在可以通过 WorkIQ 进行推理聊天。然后是您在聊天中使用的所有代理，例如研究员和分析师，甚至是我们的客户正在构建的自定义代理。最重要的是，您现在拥有编辑模式。

pairs.en: A typical trajectory or session in Copilot starts with chat: you ask questions, get insights, ask it to generate an artifact, open that artifact in Word, Excel, or PowerPoint, and further refine it—in other words, you continue the conversation. Then we now have a complete new form factor where you delegate the task—you are not even interactively working but delegating the task with CoWorkers. These are the various form factors. One of the most important things to keep in mind is the usage. It is at the same level as Outlook—this is a daily habit of intense usage. What makes these form factors useful is intelligence, which is a function of two things: multiple models coupled to context.
pairs.zh: Copilot 中的典型轨迹或会话从聊天开始：您提出问题、获得见解、要求其生成工件、在 Word、Excel 或 PowerPoint 中打开该工件，并进一步完善它 - 换句话说，您继续对话。然后，我们现在有了一个全新的形式，您可以在其中委派任务 - 您甚至不是交互式工作，而是与同事委派任务。这些是各种外形因素。要记住的最重要的事情之一是用法。它与 Outlook 处于同一级别——这是日常密集使用的习惯。这些外形因素的有用之处在于智能，它是两个因素的函数：与上下文耦合的多个模型。

pairs.en: That is meetings, emails, Teams, SharePoint data—all of that rich, constantly updated data. This is not a static database; it is the most important database in any company that is constantly changing every second. The context and the models are brought together with a harness that is multimodal. We do this in GitHub, Microsoft 365, and security. Our core goal is to decouple the harness from the models and then have the context richness show through, because customers are going to use multiple models. Critique or Counsel is a great example, or Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot. Even in Excel, I might generate using one model and check with another. That is what you want users to have access to.
pairs.zh: 即会议、电子邮件、Teams、SharePoint 数据 — 所有这些丰富的、不断更新的数据。这不是一个静态数据库；它是任何公司中最重要的数据库，并且每秒都在不断变化。上下文和模型通过多模式的线束结合在一起。我们在 GitHub、Microsoft 365 和安全性中做到这一点。我们的核心目标是将线束与模型分离，然后显示上下文的丰富性，因为客户将使用多个模型。Critique 或 Counsel 就是一个很好的例子，或者 GitHub Copilot 中的 Rubber Duck。即使在 Excel 中，我也可能使用一种模型生成并检查另一种模型。这就是您希望用户能够访问的内容。

pairs.en: Coupled with the business model of user pricing plus usage, that is what is happening, and we are seeing it play out.
pairs.zh: 再加上用户定价加使用的商业模式，这就是正在发生的事情，我们正在看到它的结果。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Thank you very much, Gabriela.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：非常感谢你，加布里埃拉。

pairs.en: Jonathan Neilson:Operator, next question, please.
pairs.zh: 乔纳森·尼尔森：接线员，请下一个问题。

pairs.en: Operator:The next question comes from the line of Kirk Materne with Evercore ISI. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 接线员：下一个问题来自 Evercore ISI 的 Kirk Materne 线路。请继续。

pairs.en: Kirk Materne:Yes, thanks very much, and thanks for taking the question. Amy, I was wondering, could you talk a little bit about the change in the OpenAI agreement—if there is anything we should be aware of from a modeling perspective or financial perspective that would change today versus where we were a couple of weeks ago? And then, Satya, it seems like an opportunity for you to continue to diversify from a model perspective. Any other takeaways we should be thinking about in terms of where you landed with OpenAI in this new framework? Thanks.
pairs.zh: Kirk Materne：是的，非常感谢，也感谢您提出问题。艾米，我想知道，您能谈谈 OpenAI 协议中的变化吗？从建模角度或财务角度来看，我们是否应该注意任何与几周前相比今天会发生变化的事情？然后，Satya，从模型的角度来看，这似乎是您继续多元化的机会。关于 OpenAI 在这个新框架中的落地，我们还应该考虑哪些其他要点？谢谢。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Maybe I will start. Overall, we feel good about our partnership with OpenAI. I am always very focused on any partnership and ensuring that there is a win-win construct at all times—that is how you can remain good partners. In this case, it starts with IP. Amy referenced this. We have a frontier model royalty-free with all the IP rights that we will have access to all the way to '32, and we fully plan to exploit it. There are examples I talked about in my remarks earlier, and we are thankful for that—that is one part of the agreement. The second part, of course, is them as a customer of ours.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：也许我会开始。总的来说，我们对与 OpenAI 的合作关系感到满意。我始终非常注重任何合作伙伴关系，并确保始终存在双赢的结构，这样才能保持良好的合作伙伴关系。在本例中，它以 IP 开头。艾米提到了这一点。我们拥有一个免版税的前沿模型，拥有我们将一直访问到 32 年的所有知识产权，并且我们完全计划利用它。我之前在发言中提到了一些例子，我们对此表示感谢——这是协议的一部分。当然，第二部分是他们作为我们的客户。

pairs.en: They are a large customer of ours, not just on the AI accelerator side, but also on all the other compute side, and we want to serve them well. And then, of course, we have our equity.
