Overall, I think the construct—as they have grown and we have grown, and our customers also have different expectations in terms of their model diversity—has evolved, but I feel very good about where we are.
pairs.zh: 他们是我们的大客户，不仅在人工智能加速器方面，而且在所有其他计算方面，我们希望为他们提供良好的服务。当然，我们还有我们的权益。总的来说，我认为随着他们的成长和我们的成长，以及我们的客户对模型多样性也有不同的期望，结构已经发生了变化，但我对我们所处的位置感到非常满意。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:The only two things to keep in mind are having the revenue share exist through 2030—the predictability of that is a real positive for us—and, as Satya pointed out, the IP being royalty-free with the elimination of our rev share to them.
pairs.zh: Amy Hood：唯一需要记住的两件事是，收入分成一直持续到 2030 年——这种可预测性对我们来说是一个真正的积极因素——而且，正如 Satya 指出的那样，IP 是免版税的，我们取消了他们的收入分成。

pairs.en: Kirk Materne:Thank you all.
pairs.zh: 柯克·马特纳：谢谢大家。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:Thanks, Kirk. Operator, we have time for one last question.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：谢谢，柯克。接线员，我们还有时间回答最后一个问题。

pairs.en: Operator:And the last question will come from the line of Rishi Jaluria with RBC Capital Markets. Please proceed.
pairs.zh: 接线员：最后一个问题将由 Rishi Jaluria 和 RBC Capital Markets 的线路提出。请继续。

pairs.en: Rishi Jaluria:Oh, wonderful. Hi, Satya. Hi, Amy. Thanks so much for squeezing me in. I wanted to go back to the discussion we have been having today on models and consumption, and the philosophy for how this changes over time. We are in complete agreement with what you are seeing out there, and it totally makes sense. I want to drill into E7, which will come out and is predominantly seat-based with some consumption components. You are doubling down on that seat element, and it seems customers still want the predictability of seat-based models, as we have seen with usage issues companies are running into as AI has gone out of control.
pairs.zh: Rishi Jaluria：哦，太棒了。嗨，萨蒂亚。嗨，艾米。非常感谢您让我参与其中。我想回到我们今天就模型和消费进行的讨论，以及随着时间的推移，这种变化的哲学。我们完全同意您所看到的情况，而且这是完全有道理的。我想深入研究 E7，它将问世，主要是基于座椅的，带有一些消费组件。你在座椅元素上加倍投入，客户似乎仍然希望基于座椅的模型具有可预测性，正如我们所看到的，随着人工智能失控，公司遇到了使用问题。

pairs.en: How do you bring these pieces together—how to maintain predictability within the customer base while increasingly growing consumption? And if we were to fast forward three to five years, how should we think about the mix of consumption versus traditional seat-based? Thanks so much.
pairs.zh: 如何将这些部分整合在一起——如何在消费不断增长的同时保持客户群的可预测性？如果我们快进三到五年，我们应该如何考虑消费与传统座位的组合？非常感谢。

pairs.en: Satya Nadella:At a high level, you said it—customers want predictability for budgets and procurement, and the seat-based pricing is just entitlement to some consumption. That is the way to think about it: there are some base usage rights that get bundled in or packaged into seats. It is a convenient way for people to buy consumption packs that happen to be assigned to seats or agents. Beyond a certain level, there are overages that go into pure consumption. Even there, if you have long-term commitments to consumption, you get appropriate discounting. That is the direction of travel. From a customer perspective, they are going to evaluate it by eval—where they are seeing the value of tokens, as simple as that.
pairs.zh: 萨蒂亚·纳德拉：您在较高层面上说过，客户希望预算和采购具有可预测性，而基于座位的定价只是某些消费的权利。这就是思考的方式：有一些基本使用权被捆绑或打包到席位中。对于人们购买恰好分配给座位或代理的消费包来说，这是一种便捷的方式。超过一定水平，就会出现超额进入纯粹消费的情况。即使在那里，如果你有长期的消费承诺，你也会得到适当的折扣。那就是行进的方向。从客户的角度来看，他们将通过 eval 来评估它——他们看到代币的价值，就这么简单。

pairs.en: Where they see the outcome—the eval on the token—whether it is improving revenue or efficiency. That will refine budgets. IT budgets will be reshaped by a combination of business outcomes making their way into IT budgets and reallocation from other line items on the income statement like OpEx.
pairs.zh: 他们在哪里看到结果——对代币的评估——无论是提高收入还是提高效率。这将完善预算。IT 预算将通过纳入 IT 预算的业务成果以及运营支出等损益表中其他项目的重新分配来重新调整。

pairs.en: Operator:Thanks, Rishi.
pairs.zh: 接线员：谢谢，Rishi。

pairs.en: Jonathan Neilson:That wraps up the Q&A portion of today's earnings call. Thank you for joining us today, and we look forward to speaking with all of you soon.
pairs.zh: 乔纳森·尼尔森：今天的财报电话会议的问答部分就到此结束。感谢您今天加入我们，我们期待很快与大家交谈。

pairs.en: Amy Hood:Thank you. Thank you.
pairs.zh: 艾米·胡德：谢谢。谢谢。

pairs.en: Operator:This concludes today's conference. You may disconnect your lines at this time, and enjoy the rest of your day.
pairs.zh: 接线员：今天的会议到此结束。此时您可以断开线路，享受剩下的一天。
